front: What are the 3 Levels of Warfare?
What is the Strategic Level of Warfare?
back: Integrates national policy and decisions into development and implementation of national defense and military strategies.

front: What is the Operational Level of Warfare?
back: Links strategy to tactics; focuses on campaigns and major operations conducted by subordinate commands.

front: What is the Tactical Level of Warfare?
back: Where battles and engagements occur to achieve military objectives assigned to Joint Force Commanders and subordinate units.

front: Which level of warfare do Airmen most commonly operate in?
What are the Air Force’s distinctive capabilities?
back:
Global Vigilance
Global Reach
Global Power
ISR—provides awareness through intelligence, surveillance, and reconnaissance.
Rapid Global Mobility—ability to move personnel, equipment, and supplies anywhere.
Global Strike—ability to project lethal force anywhere in the world.

front: Why is the Indo-Pacific region important?
back: Largest AOR; key focus on China, global trade routes, and military presence.

front: What are the 5 Air Force Core Missions?
back:
Global Strike
Air & Space Superiority
ISR
Rapid Global Mobility
Command & Control
Delivers rapid, decisive combat power (nuclear and conventional) anywhere in the world.
